law: For each alternate app icon, the project requires an Icon Composer file.
Don’t edit or remove these keys manually from the file.
Alternatively, you can use a build configuration file to specify alternate app icons.

This sample code is associated with WWDC 2019 session Use this sample code project to step through the process of building a custom modeler for GoatList, an app that displays and modifies a list of goats.
The GoatList app makes use of a pattern to break down a goal, such as sorting a list, into smaller subgoals.
This sample requires Xcode 11 or later and iOS 12 or later.
Searching for “Mobile Agent” should reveal the Custom Instrument.
You can use this movement to set up an execution environment for a , wait until certain conditions are satisfied before letting the execute at a stop, or signal failure status.
As a result, there are four possible logical steps the modeler must consider when processing a signpost: If it’s a signpost, assert a new execution bookkeeping fact.
Implementing the four steps requires the use of CLIPS Conditional Elements (CEs).
There are different mechanisms at work that make sure your modeling logic executes fully before being passed down to the rules, two of which are described below.
For example, a rule can specify that it requires a fact to have a nonempty description of the stop in which the execution occurred.
The overlapping intervals make it ambiguous as to which interval should be displayed on the lane.
To build documentation for this package, use Xcode 13, open the file, and select Product > Build Documentation.
